last_action: T009 complete — buildToc wired into updateContent's rAF block; webpack build picks up toc.ts and _toc.css

T001.did: Added slugify() helper and per-render slugCounts map in renderMarkdown; emit `id` on h1–h3 with -2/-3 collision suffix

T002.did: Created _toc.css partial — sticky aside.spec-toc, hover/focus/active states, h3 indent, --toc-min-width custom prop, narrow + empty hide hooks

T003.did: Made .content-area a flex row (display: flex, gap, align-items: flex-start) so the TOC sidebar can sit beside #markdown-content

T004.did: Removed max-width/center on .spec-header (now full-width with padding + flex-shrink:0); moved H1-suppression to body[data-has-spec-context] selector

T005.did: Added @import '_toc.css' after _content.css in the partial index

T006.did: Created toc.ts — buildToc(scrollRoot, markdownRoot, tocRoot) with smooth-scroll click handler, IntersectionObserver active tracking, ResizeObserver narrow-class toggle; idempotent teardown via WeakMap

T007.did: Added toc.ts entry, _toc.css entry, and lifted-header layout note to docs/architecture.md

T008.did: Restructured App.tsx — moved <SpecHeader /> above <main>, added <aside class='spec-toc' id='spec-toc'> inside <main>, and a useEffect that mirrors navState's spec-context presence onto body[data-has-spec-context]

T009.did: Imported buildToc in index.tsx; called it from updateContent's rAF block so the TOC rebuilds on each doc switch (and on initial template content)

approach: Pin the header above the scroll region and add a sticky TOC sidebar inside the content area, hidden via a width threshold on narrow panes.

specify.key_finding: SpecHeader currently renders inside the scrolling .content-area (not in the flex-shrink:0 .compact-nav), so it scrolls away with the markdown — making it sticky requires either moving it out of .content-area or applying position:sticky within it

plan.approach_summary: Pin the header above the scroll region and add a sticky TOC sidebar inside the content area, hidden via a width threshold on narrow panes.
risks: IntersectionObserver root must be .content-area, not the viewport — webview default-root observers silently fail
Heading-slug collisions: identical H2 text in one doc breaks anchor nav unless suffixed -2/-3 per render
Media queries don't re-fire reliably when an editor split is dragged in webviews — use ResizeObserver on .content-area instead
Lifting the header risks breaking the ~ sibling selector that hides the first H1; keep siblinghood inside .viewer-container or switch to a body[data-has-context] hook
